title: Fix: Delete Metric Fails When Categories Are Assigned
description:
Bug fix for a foreign key constraint error when deleting metrics that have assigned taxonomy categories. The metrics_taxonomies table had a foreign key to the metrics table without cascade delete, so attempting to delete a metric with category assignments would fail.

The fix updates the foreign key constraint to use `onDelete('cascade')`, allowing MySQL to automatically clean up related taxonomy assignments when a metric is deleted.
acceptance_criteria: Deleting a metric with assigned categories succeeds without errors
Related taxonomy assignments are automatically removed on metric deletion
Imported metrics can be deleted without manual cleanup
data_model_changes: Updated foreign key on metrics_taxonomies.metric_id to include onDelete('cascade'). Drops and recreates the constraint via migration.
implementation_notes: Migration drops the existing foreign key and recreates it with cascade delete. Applied to both staging and production.
comment_summary: Developer identified the foreign key constraint issue and fixed it by adding cascade delete. Applied to production and staging immediately.
